name: Calculator
description: This is another obvious contender for using the grid display type and ended up being easier than expected, thanks to the use of the "eval" function that made use of the "innerText" of the calculator buttons, with a few exceptions.

name: Timer
description: This is the first (mini) website I created and it was done,without using any JavaScript fameworks. I was getting to grips with css diplay types and was introduced to setTimeout and setInterval functions. It also introduced me to "callback functions" which are what these two functions are. Initially it didn't allow the user to input a value into the timer, but that was later added.

name: Tic Tac Toe
description: This was the first web-game that I created. I had to think of all the possible outcomes (which weren't a huge amount) when it came to checking the winner, done via another .js file. For the css, I decided to try out the grid display type, for the first time, given the asthetic nature of Tic Tac Toe.

name: Simon Says
description: This was the first website I made, to feature sound. More of the work went into the JavaScript, as opposed to the css or the hmtl. As you maybe aware, it's a game that tests one's memory by asking you to matched the sequence of sounds genereted from certain squares.

name: To Do List
description: For this website, I was exploring the addition (and removal) of elements for a list. The list items could also be edited. I also added a filter feature for the list, which was tigured by a "keydown" even. I also made use of the browser's local storage. Here, is where I implemented "Event Bubbling" so that new list items would also be subject to click events, for deletion and editing.
